mnemonics: EGGS: Ethereum's Got Gas (for smart contracts), Gwei (denomination), and Storage (for state).
keyPoints: Ether (ETH) is the native cryptocurrency used for transaction fees ('gas').
Gas is the unit that measures the computational effort required to execute operations.
The EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine) is the global, decentralized computer that executes smart contract code.
commonMistakes: Confusing Ether (the currency) with Gas (the unit of computational work).
Assuming all data is stored permanently on-chain; only state and contract storage are, not all transaction calldata.
